_status_note: verified_in_runner = fix instruction is present in source (static check) AND mirrored availability logic confirms isModify exclusion. The prompt instruction part (Claude clearing time:null) is static-check only.
title: Replace booking loop: user says 'replace it' but AI asks again
failing_behavior: After showing 'You already have a Manicure at 2 PM that day. Would you like to replace it, keep it, or pick a different time?', when user said 'replace it', the AI showed the exact same conflict question again instead of asking for a new time.
root_cause: Two issues combined: (1) System prompt said 'keep services, staff, name, phone' but didn't say to clear time:null. Claude kept time='14:00' in STATE. (2) _earlyCheckReady did not pass isModify:true when pendingAction=modify_booking, so the customer's own booking was NOT excluded from conflict checks. The same customer_conflict fired again.

conversation.content: I want a manicure at 2pm tomorrow, name is Jane, phone 4085551234

You already have a Manicure at 2:00 PM that day. Would you like to replace it, keep it, or pick a different time?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Manicure"],"staff":null,"date":"2026-04-14","time":"14:00","name":"Jane","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]

expected_no_contain: Would you like to replace it
notes: Two-part fix: (1) System prompt explicitly tells Claude to set time:null when 'replace it'. (2) JS code passes isModify:true to availability check when pendingAction=modify_booking, excluding customer's own booking. Fixed in v=20260408n.
